qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Malformed multipart requests can leave orphaned disk files and exhaust storage
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same package and release line, but this finding depends on an asynchronous fileFilter and disk-storage cleanup race that leaves orphaned files after a malformed multipart error, not the advisory's dropped-upload missing request-abort cleanup in lib/make-middleware.js.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Dropped multipart uploads leave Multer request handlers hanging and enable resource-exhaustion DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: It identifies the same Multer lib/make-middleware.js root cause fixed for GHSA-v52c-386h-88mc: client-aborted uploads are not treated as a terminal cleanup path, so the request handler can remain hung after the connection drops.

selected_finding.title: Dropped multipart uploads leave Multer request handlers hanging and enable resource-exhaustion DoS

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory-described dropped-upload bug in Multer's shared multipart middleware, and a fresh PoC run from a copied .judge workspace reproduced the hung-request condition on the pinned vulnerable v2.0.2 checkout.
evidence: The advisory summary says dropping the connection during file upload can trigger DoS; the selected finding describes the same client-abort condition and the same missing request-abort handling in repo/lib/make-middleware.js.
The upstream 2.1.0 make-middleware.js adds explicit req 'aborted' and 'close' failure handling that is absent in v2.0.2, matching the selected finding's root-cause claim.

Fresh runtime evidence in /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_f78397a8/.judge/poc_runs/request-abort-dos/run.log shows the copied PoC cloned the pinned vulnerable ref e5db9caf5da30b61e90ad56db5f6821d36ce2de9 and printed '[evohunt] HANG_CONFIRMED' after an aborted multipart upload.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC cloned Multer v2.0.2 at e5db9caf5da30b61e90ad56db5f6821d36ce2de9, ran the aborted-upload harness, and printed '[evohunt] HANG_CONFIRMED', showing the middleware callback never reached a terminal state after the client dropped the multipart upload.
